exercises.title: Type Casting
exercises.instructions:
You are given:
  x = "42"
  y = "3.14"

Convert x to a number and y to a number.
Print their sum.
exercises.hints: Use parseInt() and parseFloat() (or Number()) to convert. The result should be 45.14.

exercises.title: Local Variable: Shadows Outer
exercises.instructions: The outer x is 10. Inside show(), declare a LOCAL x so that 5 prints when show() is called.
exercises.hints: Declaring `let x` inside the function creates a new LOCAL variable that hides the outer one

exercises.title: Read an Outer Variable
exercises.instructions: A function can READ an outer variable without redeclaring it. Print the outer counter from inside show().
exercises.hints: Reading a name JavaScript doesn't find locally falls back to the enclosing/outer scope

exercises.title: Mutate an Outer Variable
exercises.instructions:
Update the OUTER count inside increment() (assign without `let`, which would make a new local).
Print count after two calls.
exercises.hints: Using `let count` inside the function makes a new local; assigning without `let` updates the outer one

exercises.title: Scope Trap: Read the Outer Variable
exercises.instructions: Fix the function so it prints the outer value 7. Just READ the outer variable — do not redeclare it with let.
exercises.hints: Declaring `let value` inside would shadow the outer one for the whole function (temporal dead zone)

exercises.title: Closure: Update an Enclosing Variable
exercises.instructions: Inside inner(), update x in the enclosing outer() scope so that outer() returns 99. (Assign without let.)
exercises.hints: A nested function can assign to a variable in its enclosing scope — just don't redeclare it with let

exercises.title: Mutability: Mutate an Array Argument
exercises.instructions: Arrays are passed by reference. Append 99 to the array inside modify() so the outer array ends as [1,2,3,99].
exercises.hints: lst refers to the same array object as nums — .push() mutates it in place

exercises.title: Mutability: Reassigning vs Mutating
exercises.instructions: Reassigning a parameter (lst = [...]) does NOT affect the caller. Print the unchanged array after rebind() runs.
exercises.hints: `lst = [...]` points the local name at a NEW array — the caller's array is untouched

exercises.title: Numbers Are Passed by Value
exercises.instructions: Reassigning a number parameter inside a function does not touch the caller. Print the unchanged outer value.
exercises.hints: Only the local name x is reassigned; n is a separate binding in the outer scope

answers.text: A named container that stores a value in memory
A fixed constant value that cannot change
